decisions.decision: Interpret 'probability input' as testing the outcome selector dropdown that displays marginal probabilities, not a slider input
alternatives_rejected: Testing a probability slider (component has none)

decisions.decision: Assert idempotencyKey with toBeDefined() without mocking crypto.randomUUID(), matching existing test conventions
Test TradeReceipt color-coding with distinct impactScore fixtures (danger >0.5, warning >0.2, muted <=0.2) via integration with parent component
alternatives_rejected: Only verifying TradeReceipt renders without checking color thresholds

decisions.decision: Test both early-return-null conditions: inactive market and unconfigured session
Assert exact formula structure using toEqual for deep matching: formula: [[{ variableId, outcomeId, negated }]]
alternatives_rejected: Partial assertion checking only key fields like accountId and side

decisions.decision: Follow exact same mocking pattern as TradingPanel.test.tsx: mock useSession and useEventTrade at module level, use vi.mocked, reuse mockMarket fixture and session fixtures, use renderWithProviders

steps.task: Create test file frontend/src/__tests__/EventTradePanel.test.tsx with module-level mocks for useSession and useEventTrade, reusing mockMarket, configuredSession, unconfiguredSession fixtures from TradingPanel pattern, and a defaultMutationState helper typed to ReturnType<typeof useEventTrade>

Write guard clause tests: (a) renders nothing when market.status is not 'active' — pass market with status 'closed', assert container.firstChild is null; (b) renders nothing when session is unconfigured — mock useSession to return unconfiguredSession, assert container.firstChild is null

Write rendering test: verify 'Quick Trade' heading, Buy/Sell toggle buttons, outcome selector with options showing marginal probabilities (e.g. 'Yes @ 65.0%'), and trade button are present when session is configured and market is active

Write interaction tests: (a) buy/sell toggle — click Sell button, verify side changes (button style or submit button text updates to 'Sell ...'); (b) outcome selection — select an outcome, verify Price label appears with correct percentage

Write submission test: select outcome 'out-yes', click trade button, assert mockMutate called once with exact payload: accountId 'acc-123', formula [[{ variableId: 'mkt-1', outcomeId: 'out-yes', negated: false }]], side 'buy', idempotencyKey toBeDefined(), and session object
Write submission with sell side test: toggle to sell, select outcome, submit, assert payload.side is 'sell'
Write pending state test: mock useEventTrade to return isPending:true, verify button shows 'Submitting...' text and is disabled

Write success state test: mock useEventTrade to return isSuccess:true with data containing order.orderId and assetDelta (beforeMinAsset, afterMinAsset, impactScore, riskLimit), verify 'Trade accepted — Order {orderId}' text and TradeReceipt values (Before, After, Impact %, Limit) are rendered

Write TradeReceipt color threshold tests: (a) impactScore 0.6 — assert impact element has style.color 'var(--color-danger)'; (b) impactScore 0.3 — assert style.color 'var(--color-warning, orange)'; (c) impactScore 0.1 — assert style.color 'var(--color-text-muted)'
Write error state test: mock useEventTrade to return isError:true with Error('Insufficient balance'), verify error message is displayed. Also test non-Error error fallback showing 'Trade failed'
Write no-submission guard test: do not select an outcome, click trade button, assert mockMutate was NOT called (handleTrade early return when !selectedOutcome)
Run vitest on the new test file to verify all tests pass: npx vitest run frontend/src/__tests__/EventTradePanel.test.tsx
